rulings.Case Title: FIVE STARS INVESTMENTS LLC VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 25, 2019 line 7. DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden re: proper termination upon landlord's default after effective notice. While Plaintiff provides evidence that a notice was provided, there is no evidence that an effective notice under the lease was provided. Plaintiff's purported admissions does not admit "effective" notice. Furthermore, there are triable issues of fact regarding Plaintiff's compliance with its obligations under the lease re: the extent and quality of the ongoing and/or periodic repairs/maintenance. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: LANCE EVANDER ET AL VS. VERITAS INVESTMENTS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 25, 2019 line 4. DEFENDANT 2619 MISSION I2, LLC, 1025 SUTTER I5, LLC, 735 O'FARRELL 17, LP, 737 PINE I1, LLC, VIC STRATEGIC MULTIFAMILY PARTNERS, LP, 430 BAKER I5 LLC, 430 BAKER I5 LP, 2824-2828 GEARY LLC, 781 O'FARRELL I7, LP SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant's motion is untimely as to Plaintiffs filing complaint and First Amended Complaint. Motion is therefore denied as it pertains to those Plaintiffs. Plaintiffs (only new Plaintiffs for the purposes of the remainder of this ruling) allege in the complaint and incorporates into every cause of action assertions that one of the Defendants' wrongful acts was levying improper pass-throughs. "Pass-through" is a term of art communicating to the defendants that they are being sued for seeking a pass-through approval by the Rent Board. Nothing in the Second Amended Complaint indicates that the "pass-through" is merely a rental increase without Rent Board's participation (e.g. Plaintiff allege that Defendants requested pass-throughs). Anti-Slapp is framed by the allegations in the Complaint. As such to the extent each cause of cation incorporates pass-through allegations (without any carve outs, e.g. 2nd cause of action), Plaintiff's claim for relief, at least in part, are based on petitioning activity. By no later than October 24, 2019 by 8:30 a.m. Plaintiffs to submit a chart indicating: "Name of Plaintiff (e.g. Adam Smith); Address of Plaintiff (e.g. 123 Lincoln Avenue); Evidence submitted in opposition showing this Plaintiff's probability of prevailing on the merits (e.g. Adam Smith declaration, paragraph 3; quote paragraph 3 in full). Courtesy copy of this brief to be delivered to department 501 no later than 8:30 a.m. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: LANCE EVANDER ET AL VS. VERITAS INVESTMENTS, INC. ET AL

rulings.Case Title: LANCE EVANDER ET AL VS. VERITAS INVESTMENTS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 25, 2019 line 4. DEFENDANT VERITAS INVESTMENTS, INC. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with final leave to amend (1) re: misjoinder. If Plaintiffs are not able to make allegations regarding "same transaction, occurrence, or series of transactions or occurrences" in good faith, Plaintiffs may re-file their actions separately. For guidance as to the required allegations to support joinder under CCP 378 see Peterson v. Bank of Am. Corp. (2014) 232 Cal.App.4th 238 discussing Moe v. Anderson (2012) 207 Cal.App.4th 862; specifically difference between medical group defendant and physician/perpetrator defendant. (2) Re: discrimination. Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of these causes of action as to each Plaintiff. See Roman v. BRE Properties (2015) 237 Cal.App.4th 1040, 1051. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL S NOUNOU VS. ANDREW LESKO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication Of The Claims
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 25, 2019 line 5. PLAINTIFF PAUL NOUNOU MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication Of The Claims DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden. Settlement Agreement provides that the parties resolved their dispute arising from the use of the Premises as both a workplace and residence. Plaintiff failed to address whether residential use is subject to SFRO; whether residential tenancy otherwise subject to SFRO may be terminated without any notice per CCP 1161(1) if it is a fixed-term tenancy; whether a 3 Day Notice (the only notice attached to the Complaint) is sufficient to terminate such tenancy; whether a 30 Day Notice (not attached to the complaint) is sufficient to terminate such tenancy, etc. =(501/CFH)
